text: During subcarinal lymph node dissection, after complete mobilization of esophagus, we could appreciate a small anomalous pulmonary vein.
The vein appeared to be draining the superior segment of the right lower lobe and entering into the pericardium directly in the subcarinal area.
Demonstration of oblique fissure confirmed its relation to the right lower lobe.
The vein was seen arching posterior to the bronchus intermedius as it entered the pericardium.
Its relation with all the mediastinal structures could be clearly made out.
This is a robotic view of the subcarinal space.
Here we are doing a subcarinal lymph node dissection during which we encountered an anomalous pulmonary vein arising from the pericardium.
which was seen arching posterior to the bronchus intermedius.
It is important to avoid injury to these veins as it can cause torrential hemorrhage.
Blind cautery for hemostasis may aggravate the bleeding due to retraction of the venous stump.
post-radiation fibrosis adds to the operative difficulty.
Hence, a meticulous dissection in the subcarinal nodal basin is imperative when such an anomalous vein is identified.
